Test Description: Verify PCIe configuration space registers for reset defaults and masked write/read behavior using multiple data patterns.
Remarks: Requires platform implementations of read_reg, write_reg, and finish. Some registers are read-only per write mask; INT_PIN uses 8-bit read mask.
Test Steps / Procedure:
1. Read each address with nonzero read_mask and compare to its default value.
2. Increment def_fail_cnt and log CFG_RST_FAIL on any default mismatch.
3. For patterns {0xAAAAAAAA, 0x55555555, 0xFFFFFFFF, 0x00000000}, write to all addresses with nonzero write_mask that are not skipped.
4. Read back each readable address and compute expected using write_mask and default.
5. Increment wr_fail_cnt and log CFG_WR_FAIL on any readback mismatch.
6. Call finish(0) if no failures; otherwise call finish(1).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass if def_fail_cnt == 0 and wr_fail_cnt == 0. Default check: if (data_rd != default_value_array[i]) { def_fail_cnt++; printf("CFG_RST_FAIL Addr:0x%x\n", addr_array[i]); } Write/read check: expected = (data_wr & write_mask_array[i]) | (~write_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i]); if (data_rd != expected) { wr_fail_cnt++; printf("CFG_WR_FAIL Addr:0x%x\n", addr_array[i]); }
Gap Analysis: Addresses and masks are hardcoded. No side-effect or reserved-bit validation beyond masks. skip_array is zero; no negative/invalid address testing.

Test Description: Validate DSP-side DBI registers for reset defaults and write/read consistency using three data patterns.
Remarks: Assumes full 32-bit R/W access via read_reg/write_reg and finish.
Test Steps / Procedure:
1. Read each address and compare with its default value.
2. Increment def_fail_cnt and log DSP_RST_FAIL on any default mismatch.
3. For patterns {0xAAAAAAAA, 0x55555555, 0x00000000}, write to all addresses.
4. Read back each address and compare to the written value.
5. Increment wr_fail_cnt and log DSP_WR_FAIL on mismatches.
6. Call finish(0) if both counters are zero; else call finish(1).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass if def_fail_cnt == 0 and wr_fail_cnt == 0. Default check: if (data_rd != default_value_array[i]) { def_fail_cnt++; printf("DSP_RST_FAIL 0x%x\n", addr_array[i]); } Write/read check: if (data_rd != data_wr) { wr_fail_cnt++; printf("DSP_WR_FAIL 0x%x\n", addr_array[i]); }
Gap Analysis: Treats all bits as fully R/W; no bitfield masking. Limited patterns; no stress or random testing.

Test Description: Check upstream-port DBI GPR registers for reset defaults and single-pattern write/read verification.
Remarks: Depends on read_reg/write_reg and finish; no console logging on mismatches.
Test Steps / Procedure:
1. Read each GPR register and compare with default value 0x00000000.
2. Increment def_fail_cnt on any default mismatch.
3. Write 0xA5A5A5A5 to each GPR register.
4. Read back each register and compare to 0xA5A5A5A5.
5. Increment wr_fail_cnt on any mismatch.
6. Call finish(0) if counters are zero; otherwise call finish(1).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass if def_fail_cnt == 0 and wr_fail_cnt == 0. Default check: if (read_reg(addr_array[i]) != default_value_array[i]) def_fail_cnt++; Write/read check: if (read_reg(addr_array[i]) != 0xA5A5A5A5) wr_fail_cnt++;
Gap Analysis: No failure logs; limited diagnosability. Only a single write pattern; no RO/bitmask handling.

Test Description: Verify SII RC inbound address mapping registers for reset defaults and two-pattern write/read behavior.
Remarks: Assumes registers are fully R/W; relies on read_reg/write_reg and finish.
Test Steps / Procedure:
1. Read each register and compare with default value 0x00000000.
2. Increment def_fail_cnt on any default mismatch.
3. For patterns {0xFFFFFFFF, 0x00000000}, write to all registers.
4. Read back each register and compare to the written value.
5. Increment wr_fail_cnt on mismatches.
6. Call finish(0) if both counters remain zero; else call finish(1).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass if def_fail_cnt == 0 and wr_fail_cnt == 0. Default check: if (read_reg(addr_array[i]) != default_value_array[i]) def_fail_cnt++; Write/read check: if (read_reg(addr_array[i]) != data_wr) wr_fail_cnt++;
Gap Analysis: No failure logging; limited observability. No handling of reserved or partial R/W fields.
